title: Agent Tool-Calling Simulation
description: Create a prompt that turns the model into a lightweight planning agent. The model will receive a user request plus a small tool catalog and should decide what tool call to make before returning a final answer. This mirrors interview questions where structure, explicit planning, and clean tool arguments matter more than writing code.
input_format: The input includes a user request and a fixed tool catalog. The model should not invent tools that are not in the catalog.
evaluator_expectation: The response must contain a plan, exactly one tool_call block with structured arguments, and a final answer. The hidden evaluator checks whether the selected tool is plausible, arguments are valid JSON, and the output is easy to parse.
input_wrapper_template:
<tool_catalog>
- create_ticket(title, description, priority)
- search_docs(query)
- schedule_meeting(title, attendees, time)
</tool_catalog>
<input>
{input}
</input>

sample_cases.name: Support escalation
sample_cases.input_data: User request: Please log a high-priority ticket for our finance team because month-end exports are failing for EU accounts.
sample_cases.expected_output:
<plan>
Use the ticket tool because the user explicitly wants an issue logged.
</plan>
<tool_call>
  <name>create_ticket</name>
  <arguments>{"title": "Month-end exports failing for EU accounts", "description": "Finance team reports failed month-end exports for EU accounts.", "priority": "high"}</arguments>
</tool_call>
<final_answer>
I would create a high-priority ticket for the finance team and include the export failure details.
</final_answer>

hidden_cases.name: Search docs request
hidden_cases.input_data: User request: Find the internal onboarding documentation for partner API authentication so I can share it with a new engineer.

hidden_cases.name: Ambiguous multi-step request
hidden_cases.input_data: User request: Set up a meeting with the platform team next Tuesday at 2pm to discuss the outage, and also file a post-mortem ticket.
hidden_cases.notes: Tests whether model picks exactly one tool and plans clearly when multiple seem relevant.

config.failure_message: The <arguments> block must contain a JSON object with key-value pairs.
The tool name must be one of: create_ticket, search_docs, schedule_meeting.
The <plan> should explain why the chosen tool is appropriate.
